# ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
## OF THE
# COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY


COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
UNITED WE STAND
DIVIDED WE FALL

Passed at the Extraordinary Session of the General Assembly, which was begun in the city of Frankfort, Kentucky, on Monday, September the nineteenth, 1960, and ended Friday, September the twenty-third, 1960.

Digitized by Google
Original from
UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA

V
S
Ky 22.1960 Ext.
FOREWORD
The 1954 General Assembly imposed upon the Legislative
Research Commission the duty of furnishing the printer correct
copy of bills and resolutions for inclusion in the Session Acts .
Section 57.180 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes stipulates that
bound copies of Session Acts be delivered for distribution within
sixty days after the end of a session .
In the preparation of this volume of the 1960 Extraordinary
Session Acts the Secretary of State has furnished access to copy of
filed enrolled bills and resolutions . The staff of the Legislative
Research Commission compared the prepared page proofs with
the enrolled bills. Mr. Courtenay J. Walker prepared the copy
for this volume .
Frankfort , Kentucky
November 1 , 1960
r
WILSON W. WYATT
Chairman
CHARLES WHEELER
Director

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE EXTRAORDINARY SESSION OF THE GENERAL
ASSEMBLY, WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY OF FRANKFORT ,
KENTUCKY , ON MONDAY , SEPTEMBER THE NINETEENΤΗ , 1960 ,
AND ENDED FRIDAY , SEPTEMBER TWENTY - THIRD , 1960 .
CHAPTER 1
(Н. В. 1)
AN ACT relating to payment of a bonus to certain veterans and certain
persons deriving from them, making an appropriation and declaring
an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. Section 40.010 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
As used in this chapter , the following terms have the follow-
ing respective meanings , unless another meaning is clearly re-
quired by the context :
( 1 ) "Administrator ” means the Adjutant General of the
Commonwealth ;
(2) "Veteran " means a person who served in the active
armed forces of the United States , during the Spanish American
War , World War I , World War II , or the Korean conflict , for a
period of ninety days or more ( exclusive of time spent AWOL ; or
in penal confinement as a result of a sentence imposed by court
martial ; or in service for which no allowance is made according
to KRS 40.040 ) , with some portion of service within the respec-
tive hereinafter prescribed dates , who is still in the armed forces ,
or was released , separated , discharged or retired therefrom under
honorable conditions ;
(3) "Duty in active armed forces " includes active duty , and
any period of inactive duty training during which the individual
concerned was disabled ; and if a person in the active armed
forces was released , separated or discharged therefrom by reason
of disability incurred in line of duty before serving as much as
ninety days , such person shall be qualified for entitlement to a
bonus payment under this chapter , notwithstanding failure to

2 ACTS , FIRST EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
remain in service for the minimum time otherwise prescribed ;
(4) "Armed forces" means the United States Army, Navy,
Marine Corps , Air Force and Coast Guard , including the reserve
components thereof on active duty ;
(5 ) “ Qualified veteran " means a person answering to the
specifications set forth in subsections ( 2 ) and ( 3 ) , and who
( a) Was a resident of the Commonwealth at the time of
entry into active service in the armed forces and for at least six
months prior thereto ; and
(b) Who has not received a bonus or like compensation
from another state ; and
( c) Who is not subject to the forfeiture provisions of this
chapter ;
( 6) "Resident of the Commonwealth at the time of entry
into the active service ” means any person who gave the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky , or any specific place in this Common-
wealth , as his or her place of residence at such time of entry ,
without regard to the place of enlistment , commission , or induc-
tion . Conclusive and exclusive evidence of such giving of place
of residence shall be the official records on file in the Depart-
ment of Defense of the United States , or any official record there-
of in the files of the Veterans' Administration of the United
States ; but if it be shown to the satisfaction of the administrator
that for any reason no such record was made, or that the same
has been lost , misplaced or destroyed , or that an authenticated
copy thereof cannot be obtained within a reasonable time , other
evidence of bona fide residence may be accepted if deemed suf-
ficient by the administrator ;
(7) "Resident ," in any context other than as in subsection
( 6) , means a legal resident as determined by generally estab-
lished principles of law, as may be defined , and subject to proof ,
according to such regulations as the administrator may promul-
gate .
( 8 ) "Beneficiary " means in this order , widow , child or chil-
dren ( sharing equally ) , mother , father , and no other ;
( 9 ) ( a ) "Widow " means a woman who was the wife of a
veteran at the time of his death , and who had not deserted him
(except where there was a separation which was due to the mis-
conduct of, or procured by, the veteran without the fault of the

re-

(b) The term

"Spanish American War
"

4 ACTS , FIRST EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
( a) Means the period beginning on April 21, 1898 , and
ending on July 4, 1902 ;
(b ) Includes the Philippine Insurrection and the Boxer Re-
bellion ; and
( c) In the case of a veteran who served with the United
States military forces engaged in hostilities in the Moro Province ,
means the period beginning on April 21, 1898 , and ending on
July 15, 1903 ;
( 16 ) " World War I "
( a) Means the period beginning on April 6, 1917 , and end-
ing on November 11 , 1918 ; and
(b) In the case of a veteran who served with the United
States military forces in Russia , means the period beginning on
April 6, 1917 , and ending on April 1, 1920 ; and
(c ) Any service between April 6, 1917 , and July 1 , 1921 ,
if some part thereof was between April 6, 1917 , and November
11 , 1918 , both dates being inclusive ;
( 17 ) "World War II " means the period beginning Decem-
ber 7, 1941 , and ending December 31 , 1946 ;
( 18 ) " Korean conflict " means
( a ) The period beginning on June 27, 1950 , and ending
July 25, 1953 ; and
(b ) Subsequent service until January 31 , 1955 , if the vet-
eran was qualified for the Korean Medal ;
( 19 ) " Bonus ” and “veterans' bonus " mean the compensa-
tion authorized by this chapter ;
( 20 ) "Bonus claim " means a claim or potential claim for a
veterans ' bonus ;
( 21 ) “ Claimant " means one who seeks to obtain payment
of a bonus claim .
Section 2. In addition to the sums heretofore appropriated
for the administration for the veterans bonus act , there is hereby
appropriated an additional sum of $630,000 for the administration
of said act . This includes the additional amounts necessary for
state agencies involved , including the departments of Finance ,
Revenue , Treasury and Adjutant General to administer this act .
No part thereof shall lapse at the end of the fiscal year 1960-61
but any remaining balances shall be carried forward for use in
the subsequent fiscal year .

CHAPTER 2 5
Section 3. It is found and declared that public interest
requires prompt payment of the veterans bonus to all qualified
applicants ; and to that end it is essential that the Administrator
continue to receive and process claims as expeditiously as possi-
ble . Accordingly an emergency is declared to exist and this Act
shall be effective upon
itspassage and approval by the Governor
.
Approved October
1, 1960

service , including drugs and medical supplies , appliances ,
laboratory , diagnostic and therapeutic services , nursing home
and convalescent care, hospital care as defined in KRS 205.560
(1) (a), and such other essential medical services and supplies as
may be prescribed by such persons ;
(5) “Nurses ” means persons authorized to practice profes-
sional nursing under the laws of the Commonwealth ;
(6) "Nursing Home " means a facility which provides routine
medical care in which physicians regularly visit patients , which
provide nursing services and procedures employed in caring for
the sick which require training , judgment , technical knowledge ,
and skills beyond that which the untrained person possesses , and
which maintains complete records on patient care, and which is
licensed pursuant to the provisions of KRS 216.400 ;
(7) "Pharmacist " means a person authorized to practice
pharmacy under the laws of the Commonwealth ;
(8) "Physician " means a person authorized to practice
medicine or osteopathy under the laws of the Commonwealth ;
(9) "Public Assistance Recipient " means a person who has
been certified by the Division of Public Assistance of the De-
partment of Economic Security as being eligible for, and a
recipient of, public assistance under the provisions of KRS
205.010 to 205.340 or a needy person who is living with and es-
sential to such recipient and is recognized in the determination
of budgetary requirements for such payment ;
(10) "Recipient of Medical Assistance for the Aged" means
an aged person sixty- five years of age and over and one who
re-

CHAPTER 2 7
( 11 ) "Vendor payment " means a payment for medical care
which is paid by the Department of Economic Security directly
to the authorized person or institution which rendered medical
care to a public assistance recipient or medical care to a recipient
of medical assistance to the aged .
Section 2. Section 205.550 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
The council shall develop and formulate recommendations
to the Department of Economic Security and the Department of
Health as to policies , plans , budgets , programs , and rules and
regulations relating to:
( 1) The administration of KRS 205.510 to 205.610 , 205.991
and 211.106 ;
(2) The utilization of facilities and personnel of the De-
partment of Economic Security and the Department of Health ;
(3) The designation , by regulation , of the scope of medical
care for which the Department of Economic Security should
undertake to pay ;
(4) The designation , by regulation , of quantity and quality
standards and of methods for determining costs for vendor pay-
ments for medical care services , taking into consideration insur-
ance and other financial resources available to the recipients ;
(5) The designation , by regulation , of the limitations , dura-
tion and frequency of medical care for which vendor payments
for medical care services should be made ;
(6) The administration of grants , gifts , and contributions
from the Federal Government , or from other sources , for the
purpose of carrying out the intent and purposes of KRS 205.510
to 205.610 , 205.991 and 211.106 ;
(7) The establishment of and contribution to a federal-
state medical care fund from funds appropriated from time to
time by the General Assembly ;
(8) The conducting of studies , research , demonstrations ,
and surveys to carry out the purposes of KRS 205.510 to 205.610 ,
205.991 and 211.106 ;
• (9) Agreements and contracts for the administration of KRS
205.510 to 205.610 , 205.991 and 211.106 ;

8 ACTS , FIRST EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
(10) The utilization of facilities and personnel of local
health departments and local public assistance offices ;
(11 ) The periodic evaluation of the program and publica-
tion of reports thereof ;
(12) The formulation of such modifications , expansions , or
contractions of the program as may be desirable or necessary .
Section 3. Section 205.560 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1) The scope of medical care for which the Department
of Economic Security undertakes to pay shall be designated and
limited by regulations promulgated by said department , pur-
suant to the provisions hereof . The selection of the class or
classes of medical care shall be recommended by the council to
the Commissioner of the Department of Economic Security and
the Commissioner of the Department of Health after taking into
consideration , among other things , the amount of federal and
state funds available , the most essential needs of public assist-
ance recipients and recipients of medical assistance for the aged,
and the meeting of such need on a basis insuring the greatest
amount of medical care as defined in KRS 205.510 consonant
with the funds available including but not limited to the follow-
ing categories :
(a) Hospital care, including drugs , and medical supplies
and services during any period of actual hospitalization ;
(b) Nursing home care, including medical supplies and
services , and drugs during confinement therein on prescription
of a physician or dentist ;
(c) Drugs, nursing care, and medical supplies and services
during the time when a recipient is not in a hospital but is under
treatment and on the prescription of a physician or dentist ;
(d) Physician and dental services .
(2) Payments for hospital care, nursing home care , and
drugs or other medical and dental supplies shall be on bases
which relate the amount of the payment to the cost of providing
such services or supplies . It shall be one of the functions of the
council to make recommendations to the Department of Health
with respect to such bases for payment .

CHAPTER 2 9
(3) No vendor payment shall be made unless the class and
type of medical care rendered and the cost basis therefor has first
been designated by regulation .
(4) The range of medical care benefit standards provided
and the quality and quantity standards and the methods for
determining cost formulae for vendor payments within each
category of public assistance and other recipients shall be uni-
form for the entire state , and shall be designated by regulation
promulgated within the limitations established by the Medical
Assistance Act of 1960 , as amended herein , and federal law ,
including the Social Security Amendments of 1960. The amount
of payments for units of services need not be uniform for the
entire state but may vary from county to county and from city
to city , as well as among hospitals , based on the prevailing cost of
medical care in such locale and other local economic and geo-
graphic conditions .
Section 4. Section 205.570 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1) The Department of Economic Security shall retain

10 ACTS , FIRST EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
known as the "Social Security Amendments of 1960 ," and any
amendment made thereto subsequent to 1960 .
Section 5. Section 205.610 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
Payments into the federal -state medical care fund shall be
dedicated to the payment of costs of medical care for public
assistance recipients and recipients of medical assistance for the
aged . Federal -state medical care funds shall be subject to fed-
eral participation as an assistance expenditure for medical care
in behalf of public assistance recipients and recipients of medical
assistance for the aged.
Section 6. (1) Notwithstanding the provisions of Subsec-
tion 3, Part Four , and other sections , of Chapter 45 of the 1960
Acts of the General Assembly (the 1960 Budget Bill), there is
hereby appropriated to the Department of Economic Security out
of the General Fund of the State Treasury , out of funds unex-
pended whether or not heretofore appropriated to the Depart-
ment of Finance for the Capital Construction Account, the fol-
lowing amounts for the fiscal years stated :
1960-1961
1961-1962
$325,000
$600,000
(2) The sums herein appropriated shall be used to defray
the costs of administration and the costs of benefits , and may
include payment to the Department of Health for services
rendered under contract . Any funds remaining at the end of
the first year of the biennium shall be carried forward into the
second year of the biennium .
Section 7. Nothing in the Act shall be interpreted to require
the payment of any benefits hereunder before January 1 , 1961 .
Section 8. Inasmuch as the planning necessary to secure
the implementation of this Act should begin at once, an emer-
gency is declared to exist, and this Act shall become effective
upon
itsapproval
bythe Governor
.
Approved October
1, 1960

CHAPTER 3 11
CHAPTER 3
(H. R. 3 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION appropriating money for the 1960 Extraordinary
Session of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 1. That there is hereby appropriated out of the
General Expenditure Fund for the payment of salaries and
other expenses of the 1960 Extraordinary Session of the General
Assembly the sum of sixty-five thousand ( $65,000 ) dollars .
Section 2. That whereas funds heretofore appropriated for
sessions of the General Assembly are exhausted, an emergency is
declared to exist and this resolution shall become effective im-
mediately upon
itspassage and approval by the Governor
.
Approved September 23
, 1960
